what_it_does: A generated ledger that maps UI regions and their hierarchical ui:// addresses to the React component files that render them, distinguishing implemented (built) surfaces from unimplemented (planned) ones, and lists shared components.
observations: 35 regions are listed, with only 10 marked built and 25 marked planned
Built components point to files under canvas/app/src/regions/, canvas/app/src/App.tsx, or canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx
Planned regions mostly carry the note 'region in addresses.json with no component mapping'
Addresses are deeply nested namespaces, e.g. ui://inbox/build-review/... and ui://canvas/node/...
Some ui:// addresses appear in more than one region, such as ui://walkthrough and ui://twin
Shared components are listed separately from regions and include PanelView, NodeShape, and BuildIntentCard
The file declares it is produced by blueprint_emit.py and validated by blueprint_acceptance.py
standouts: Explicit 'planned, not fiction' honesty about unbuilt UI surfaces
A large, hierarchical ui:// address space is the primary organizing key
The canvas region is rendered by App.tsx and NodeShape.tsx rather than a dedicated region component
Palette.tsx is the implementation file for the rail region
conventions: Top-level _what field documents provenance and generation script
Each region has region, component, status, note, and addresses fields
Component paths are repository-relative file paths
ui:// addresses use slash-delimited hierarchical namespaces
Shared components are tracked in a separate array with name, component, and status
concerns: Planned regions outnumber built ones by a wide margin
Some ui:// addresses are duplicated across regions, creating ownership ambiguity
Notes for planned regions are often identical and generic
No schema version, timestamp, or priority/ordering metadata is present
The rail region is implemented by Palette.tsx, which may confuse naming expectations
notes: A generated, honest map of which UI regions and ui:// addresses already have React components and which are still planned.
questions: What exactly does the F0 carve refer to?
How are ui:// addresses assigned and guaranteed to be unique across regions?
What criteria move a region from planned to built?
How does blueprint_acceptance.py verify that built component files exist?
Why are some addresses like ui://walkthrough and ui://twin listed under both chat and their own regions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a generated inventory linking UI regions/addresses to components and honestly marking implementation status.
intention_signals: The file appears designed to make the gap between the design address space and actual code visible and auditable, so the team can track which UI surfaces are real and which remain aspirational.
novelty: The use of a hierarchical ui:// address ledger tied to region components, with an explicit 'planned, not fiction' status for unbuilt surfaces, is distinct from a typical component index.
contribution: Provides a structured, status-aware index between UI addresses and rendering components, useful for build acceptance, gap analysis, and navigation without conflating planned and implemented surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A generated JSON inventory mapping UI regions and hierarchical ui:// addresses to their React component files, marking each as built or planned, plus a list of shared components.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a transparent, machine-generated ledger of which UI surfaces have corresponding React implementations and which are still planned.
